name: Hawaii (United States)
description: Eight equal horizontal stripes alternating white, red, and blue (top to bottom: white, red, blue, white, red, blue, white, red), with a British Union Jack in the upper-hoist canton occupying roughly the upper quarter of the flag.
history: Designed during the reign of King Kamehameha I, c. 1816, combining elements of the British and U.S. flags to signal Hawaii's diplomatic alignment with both powers; one tradition holds that an early version was given to Kamehameha I by Captain George Vancouver. Adopted in its current form by Kamehameha I in 1845; retained without change through the Hawaiian Kingdom (until 1893), the Republic of Hawaii (1894-1898), the Territory of Hawaii (1898-1959), and the U.S. State of Hawaii (since 21 August 1959). It is the only U.S. state flag to incorporate the flag of a foreign country.

features.symbolism: the eight major islands of the Hawaiian archipelago

features.role: British Union Jack canton in the upper hoist quarter
features.symbolism: Hawaii's historical friendship with the United Kingdom; gifted by Captain George Vancouver to King Kamehameha I in the 1790s
